commands.review_summary:
The PR adds linux-musl x64/aarch64 release matrix entries, installs and prunes the Swift Static Linux SDK, builds static SQLite in CI, and carries the stacked Musl source/linker changes from related PRs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a PR review; the linked Linux portability issue is source/release-artifact reproducible, and current main lacks the musl artifact path this branch adds.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is needed for release/toolchain policy and stacked merge order; there is no narrow code defect for a repair worker.

target.title: Add musl CLI artifacts to release workflow
target.body:
Refs #1524

This is PR 3 in the staged static Linux CLI work. It adds release workflow coverage for Swift Static Linux SDK / musl CLI artifacts.

Stacked on #1601 and #1609. Until those merge, this PR includes their commits plus the release workflow change here.

## Root cause

The current release CLI workflow publishes macOS artifacts and Linux CLI artifacts built on glibc runners with `--static-swift-stdlib`. Those Linux artifacts are not fully static musl builds, so they do not cover older glibc / musl / NixOS-style runtime portability goals from #1524.

While validating the first workflow version, the Swift Static Linux SDK artifactbundle exposed both `x86_64-swift-linux-musl` and `aarch64-swift-linux-musl` metadata to SwiftPM. In GitHub Actions, that allowed SwiftPM to select or report Swift modules from the opposite SDK architecture. This PR now prunes the installed SDK metadata and sysroot directories per matrix job before building.

## Changes

- Keeps existing `linux-x64`, `linux-arm64`, and macOS release artifacts.
- Adds release matrix entries for:
  - `linux-musl-x64`
  - `linux-musl-arm64`
- Installs the Swift Static Linux SDK in musl matrix jobs.
- Narrows the installed SDK to the current matrix `swift-sdk-triple` / `swift-sdk-arch` before invoking SwiftPM.
- Builds a temporary target-architecture static `libsqlite3.a` from the official SQLite amalgamation during the workflow.
- Uses `CODEXBAR_SQLITE3_LIB_DIR` from #1609 so SwiftPM can satisfy SweetCookieKit's `-lsqlite3` during static SDK linking.
- Builds musl CLI artifacts with `--swift-sdk` and explicit musl triples.
- Packages new artifacts as:
  - `CodexBarCLI-{tag}-linux-musl-x86_64.tar.gz`
  - `CodexBarCLI-{tag}-linux-musl-aarch64.tar.gz`
- Updates Linux `file` smoke checks to use file-output arch patterns (`x86-64` vs `x86_64`).

Explicitly not included:

- No SQLite source is vendored into this repository.
- No SweetCookieKit changes.
- No Homebrew tap alias switch to musl artifacts in this PR.
- No removal of existing glibc Linux artifacts.

## Verification

Local checks:

```bash
ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-cli.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'
git diff --check
```

Result:

```text
yaml ok
git diff --check: passed
```

Local x86_64 musl static build smoke for the underlying command path, using a copied/pruned Swift Static Linux SDK:

```bash
env CODEXBAR_SQLITE3_LIB_DIR=/private/tmp/codexbar-sqlite-musl/lib \
  HOME=/private/tmp/codexbar-1524/.home-pr3-pruned-escalated \
  CLANG_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/private/tmp/codexbar-1524/.clang-module-cache-pr3-pruned-escalated \
  ~/Library/Developer/Toolchains/swift-6.2.1-RELEASE.xctoolchain/usr/bin/swift \
  build --scratch-path /private/tmp/codexbar-1524-build-pr3-pruned-x86-escalated \
  --swift-sdks-path "$tmp_sdk_root/swift-sdks" \
  -c release --product CodexBarCLI \
  --swift-sdk swift-6.2.1-RELEASE_static-linux-0.0.1 \
  --triple x86_64-swift-linux-musl
```

Result:

```text
Build of product 'CodexBarCLI' complete!
```

`file` result for the local x86_64 musl binary:

```text
ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), statically linked
```

Manual release workflow validation on fork:

- Run: https://github.com/Yuxin-Qiao/CodexBar/actions/runs/27735285990
- Head SHA: `a3e2175216af9a929781bac70e71dafa1885daf3`
- Result: success
- Covered release jobs:
  - `linux-musl-x64`: build, smoke test, package, artifact upload
  - `linux-musl-arm64`: build, smoke test, package, artifact upload
  - existing `linux-x64`, `linux-arm64`, `macos-x86_64`, and `macos-arm64`

Notes:

- The local machine is macOS, so Linux ELF execution was verified by the release workflow smoke tests on Linux runners.
- `aarch64-swift-linux-musl` was verified by the `ubuntu-24.04-arm` release matrix job in the manual workflow run above.
target.author: yuxin-qiao
